output:
(B) is correct.

This is a textbook **confounded causal inference**. The manager observed a correlation (agile team → more features) and jumped to a causal conclusion (agile → more productivity) without controlling for confounders.

Teams differ in many ways beyond methodology — skill levels, project complexity, domain, tech debt, team size, stakeholder stability. Any of these could explain part or all of the 40% gap. You can't isolate methodology's effect from a single uncontrolled comparison of two teams.
